name: Valley Educators
description_short: Valley Educators is a federally insured state-chartered credit union in Alamosa, CO serving 1,016 members with $5.98M in assets since 1959.
description_long:
Valley Educators Credit Union was established in 1959 and has operated as a federally insured state-chartered credit union for 67 years. Based in Alamosa, Colorado, the institution serves a membership of approximately 1,016 people with a single branch location and a lean operational structure of 3 full and part-time employees. The credit union maintains total assets of $5.98 million as of September 30, 2025, with governance led by CEO/Manager Laura Schell and Chairperson Lisa Absmeier.

The credit union offers core banking and financial services typical of community-focused credit unions, including checking and savings accounts, ATM/Debit Card Program access, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio reflects a diversified approach, with $4.5M in total loans distributed across real estate loans ($1.12M), unsecured loans ($77K), and other loan categories ($3.34M). The organization maintains $4.6M in shares and deposits from members and reported net income of $50,446 in their most recent reporting period.

As a member-owned, not-for-profit institution, Valley Educators operates under a natural person membership model and maintains NCUA insurance protection for member deposits. The credit union is not affiliated with the Federal Home Loan Bank system and does not hold a low-income credit union designation. Their conservative balance sheet shows strong equity of $1.33M against total liabilities of $4.6M, indicating financial stability appropriate for a small community credit union.

Valley Educators serves a niche membership in the San Luis Valley region and is best suited for local residents seeking personalized service from a small, community-based financial institution. However, as a micro-credit union with minimal assets and a single location, members should be aware of limited service expansion, fewer technology resources compared to larger credit unions, and potentially narrower product offerings. The institution's longevity and member focus make it appropriate for those prioritizing local ownership and personalized relationships over comprehensive service breadth.
services: All Other Shares & Deposits (alternative deposit products)
pros: Federally insured state-chartered credit union with 67-year operational history and NCUA protection
Low-cost, member-owned not-for-profit structure with net income reinvested into member services
Strong equity position of $1.33M providing financial stability and safety for deposits
ATM/Debit Card Program offering card-based access to funds
Diverse loan portfolio including real estate, unsecured, and other loan types
Local presence in Alamosa with direct community connection and personalized service
Low Cost Wire Transfers advertised as available service
cons: Micro-institution with only $5.98M in assets limits service expansion and product development
Single branch location provides no geographic flexibility for members outside Alamosa area
Minimal digital presence—website redirects to itsme247.com with no dedicated credit union domain
Very small staff (3 employees) may limit availability and response times during business hours
Limited online banking/technology infrastructure compared to regional credit unions
best_for: Alamosa, CO residents seeking community-based banking with local ownership
Educators and local workforce members seeking personalized member service
People prioritizing relationship banking and small institution values over technology features
Members comfortable with single-location access and basic banking products
diagnosis: Valley Educators is best for Alamosa residents and local workforce members who value community banking relationships and member ownership over technological sophistication or service breadth. The main caveat is that as a micro-credit union with $6M in assets and a single location, members should expect limited product innovation, minimal digital services, and geographic constraints that make it unsuitable for anyone relocating or requiring multi-state access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
